reasoning: I will provide a concise list of common symptoms associated with a nonfunctioning gallbladder (biliary dyskinesia) and note variability, as well as when to seek care.
output:
- Recurrent pain in the upper right abdomen, especially after meals
- Epigastric or abdominal pain that may last from 30 minutes to several hours
- Pain that may radiate to the back between the shoulder blades or to the right shoulder
- Indigestion, including bloating, gas, and heartburn
- Nausea and sometimes vomiting
- Feeling of fullness or intolerance to fatty foods
- Occasional fever or chills if inflammation or infection is present (less common)
- Some people may have no noticeable symptoms, and dysfunction is found incidentally
